title: Automated individual decision-making
summary: Creates safeguards around decisions based solely on automated processing that produce legal or similarly significant effects.

title: Automated decision-making safeguards
summary: Requires transparency, fairness and impartiality in automated decision-making and provides specific protections in significant decisions and marketing.

title: Transparency for high-risk AI
summary: Requires high-risk AI systems to be sufficiently transparent for deployers to interpret outputs and use the system appropriately.
applicationStatus: Generally applies from 2 August 2026; consult Article 113

title: Transparency and explainability
summary: Calls for meaningful disclosure about AI systems and information that helps affected people understand and challenge outcomes.

title: Document system knowledge limits
summary: Documents an AI system’s knowledge limits and how human actors may use and oversee its outputs.

title: Data protection impact assessment
summary: Requires an impact assessment where processing is likely to create a high risk to individuals’ rights and freedoms.

title: Personal information protection impact assessment
summary: Requires prior impact assessments for sensitive information, automated decisions, entrusted processing, transfers and other high-impact activities.

title: Risk management system
summary: Requires a continuous, iterative risk-management process across the lifecycle of a high-risk AI system.
applicationStatus: Generally applies from 2 August 2026; consult Article 113

title: Prioritize and manage AI risks
summary: Provides outcomes for prioritizing assessed risks, planning responses and monitoring whether treatments remain effective.

title: Robustness, security and safety
summary: Calls for lifecycle risk management, traceability and mechanisms to address AI systems that create undue harm.

title: Cybersecurity risk-management measures
summary: Requires proportionate technical, operational and organisational measures to manage network and information-system risks.
applicationStatus: EU directive; consult the relevant national implementing law

title: Breach notice to supervisory authority
summary: Requires controllers to notify the supervisory authority of qualifying personal-data breaches, generally within 72 hours.

title: Personal-information incident response
summary: Requires immediate remedial measures and notice to the responsible authority and affected individuals, subject to stated conditions.

title: Significant-incident reporting
summary: Creates a staged reporting process for significant incidents, including early warning, notification and a final report.
applicationStatus: EU directive; consult the relevant national implementing law

title: Security-breach notification
summary: Requires notice to California residents following qualifying breaches of specified personal information, subject to statutory conditions.

title: Network-data breach response
summary: Requires remedial action and notice where personal information handled by a network operator is leaked, damaged or lost.

title: Take action on detected incidents
summary: Organizes outcomes for incident management, analysis, reporting, communication and mitigation.

title: General principle for international transfers
summary: Makes transfers to third countries subject to the conditions in GDPR Chapter V while preserving the Regulation’s level of protection.

title: Mechanisms for overseas provision
summary: Lists legal mechanisms for providing personal information outside China, including assessment, certification and standard-contract routes.

title: Notice and separate consent for overseas provision
summary: Requires specified notice about the overseas recipient and separate consent when consent is the applicable processing basis.

title: Transborder data flows
summary: Calls on members to consider other countries’ observance of the Guidelines and avoid unjustified restrictions on transborder personal-data flows.

title: Security of processing
summary: Requires risk-appropriate technical and organisational security measures for personal-data processing.

title: Personal-information security measures
summary: Requires measures proportionate to processing purpose, method, information type, impact and security risks.

title: Accuracy, robustness and cybersecurity
summary: Requires high-risk AI systems to achieve appropriate accuracy, robustness and cybersecurity throughout their lifecycle.
applicationStatus: Generally applies from 2 August 2026; consult Article 113

title: Multi-level protection duties
summary: Requires network operators to perform security-protection duties under the classified cybersecurity protection system.

title: Use safeguards to manage cyber risk
summary: Organizes protective outcomes including access control, data security, platform security and infrastructure resilience.
